title: Today's Tech Brief: UK banks offered access to OpenAI’s GPT-5.5 amid exclusion from Anthropic’s Glasswing expansion
summary: UK banks get access to OpenAI's GPT-5.5, while China approves the world's first invasive brain-computer chip.

imageSourceTitle: UK banks offered access to OpenAI’s GPT-5.5 amid exclusion from Anthropic’s Glasswing expansion

body.text: I've got a couple of interesting tech updates for you today. First, UK banks are being offered access to OpenAI's GPT-5.5. This is a big deal because GPT-5.5 is one of the most advanced language models out there, capable of understanding and generating human-like text. What's interesting here is that UK banks are getting access to this tech while being excluded from Anthropic's Glasswing expansion. It seems like there's a bit of a tech divide happening here.
A closer look at the UK banks and GPT-5.5

So, what does this mean for UK banks? With GPT-5.5, they can automate certain tasks, like customer service and data analysis. This could lead to more efficient operations and better customer experiences. I'm curious to see how they'll use this tech to improve their services. For instance, will they use it to build more conversational interfaces or to analyze large datasets?

UK banks offered access to OpenAI’s GPT-5.5 amid exclusion from Anthropic’s Glasswing expansion is also worth a quick mention. The point is not to force it into a giant theory; it is simply part of today's news mix.

My quick read is that today's set of stories is useful because it gives us texture, not a sermon. Each item has its own little job: one tells us what changed, another tells us what people are worried about, and another gives the day its mood.
